body:
Dear Verda Team,

I am applying for the AI Developer Advocate role. I noticed this position while researching companies at the intersection of AI infrastructure and developer experience, and Verda's approach to the full ML lifecycle stood out as exactly the kind of platform I want to help developers adopt.

I build developer communities around systems that do not have documentation yet. I have done this for Bitcoin Ordinals when it was 3 months old, for passkey wallet infrastructure before the spec was finalised, and for an entire game engine ecosystem where I shipped 535 commits across 10 repositories in 16 days, producing documentation, SDKs, and API specifications alongside the code.

Your listing asks for someone who creates deep-dive technical content that AI developers trust, builds reproducible assets they can clone and run, and represents Verda at events. That is what I do, and I have the public portfolio to prove it.

Specific evidence mapped to your requirements:

- 3+ years public-facing DevRel output — 21 presentations on SlideShare (59K+ views), TEDx speaker, conference talks at ASIFMA/Thomson Reuters, multi-city workshop tours across Europe. Active technical writing (protocol whitepapers, developer tutorials, ecosystem articles).

- Technical content developers engage with — "Introducing Bitcoin" presentation (31.5K views), SADO Protocol documentation, Ordit SDK (~50K npm downloads), Everstore whitepaper (25 pages). I write for engineers who build.

- Reproducible technical assets — Built and published: open-source SDKs, MCP server with 16 AI-callable tools, OpenAPI specifications, REST APIs, llms.txt integration, consumer-facing developer documentation. Everything I produce is cloneable and runnable.

- Simplifying complex infrastructure without losing precision — My entire career is translating protocol-level systems (UTXO scripting, Schnorr/Taproot, cryptographic key derivation, distributed consensus) into practical developer resources. GPU infrastructure and ML frameworks are a new domain; the translation skill is the same.

- Self-driven editorial calendar — Currently running a content pipeline across multiple platforms with automated scheduling, cross-project triggers, and measurable engagement tracking. This is operational, not aspirational.

On the GPU/ML dimension: I am not a PyTorch practitioner today. What I am is someone who repeatedly goes from zero to producing trusted technical content in complex infrastructure domains in weeks, not months. At CakeDefi, I went from knowing nothing about Ordinals to co-authoring the SADO Protocol and shipping production systems within my first quarter. At Moddable, I went from concept to full ecosystem (engines, SDKs, 16 MCP tools, documentation site) in 16 days. Working alongside your AI Lab, I would expect the same ramp on ML systems, and my first published content would arrive from the perspective your audience actually needs: someone who just learned it and can explain it clearly.

I am based in the UK, available immediately, and open to hybrid London or remote EU. My portfolio is at smalley.my and I have attached my CV for reference.

Kind regards,
Mark Smalley
